basic_info.title: Terranigma
basic_info.review: A masterpiece of the SNES library, praised for its beautiful art style, deep gameplay, and unique world-restoration mechanics that set it apart from contemporaries.

alternative_names: The Creation of Heaven and Earth

previous_game.title: Landstalker: The Challenging Quest of Leo and Lina

similar_games.title: Zelda II: The Adventure of Link

play_today.details: Original PAL version without patches. Runs at 50fps with original English localization.

recommended_patches.name: NTSC Patch
recommended_patches.description: Converts PAL version to NTSC format for 60fps gameplay.

optimal_settings: Standard accuracy mode
Default audio settings
Auto-save enabled
Custom overlay for touchscreen controls

play_today.details: The Australian SNES version is identical to the European release in terms of content and language barriers. It is ranked third as it offers no unique advantages over the European or Japanese versions. Like the other SNES variants, it provides the best technical performance and visual fidelity of the game's history. However, without the Redux patch, it remains inaccessible to English speakers, making it a lower priority than the Japanese version which has a more established patching community.

recommended_patches.name: Terranigma Redux + Retranslation
recommended_patches.description: A comprehensive patch for the SNES version that provides a complete English retranslation, fixes text spacing issues, rebalances weapons and bosses, and includes NTSC conversion for 60fps gameplay.

recommended_patches.name: Terranigma NTSC Patch
recommended_patches.description: Converts the PAL SNES version of Terranigma to NTSC format, allowing the game to run at 60fps instead of 50fps for smoother gameplay and correct timing.

recommended_patches.name: Terranigma Practise
recommended_patches.description: Adds a practice menu to the SNES version allowing players to set their level, items, and location for speedrunning or testing specific game mechanics.

recommended_patches.name: Terranigma Blue: Ark's Apology
recommended_patches.description: A quality-of-life patch for the SNES version that corrects Ark's sprite to match original concept art and nerfs the HP of the boss Bloody Mary for better balance.

optimal_settings: Enable High-Precision Audio for accurate sound reproduction
Set Internal Resolution to 4x or 8x for sharp scaling
Enable CRT Filter (CRT-Royale) for authentic scanline look
Set Video Mode to 'NTSC' for correct color timing
Enable SuperFX chip emulation if applicable
Set Resolution to 2x or 3x internal scaling
Use CRT shader for authentic look
Enable Audio Interpolation for smoother sound
Enable touch controls with custom layout
Set audio interpolation to high
Enable save state support for quick loading
Set screen scaling to 'Stretch' or 'Fit' based on device
Core: Snes9x or bsnes-mercury
Shader: CRT-Royale or Scanlines
Save state support enabled
Input remapping for controller support

description.synopsis: In a world where the land has turned to mud and life has ceased, a young boy named Ark awakens with the power to restore the earth. Guided by a mysterious voice, Ark travels across the globe, reviving dead creatures and healing the land to uncover the truth behind the world's destruction and the ancient civilization that once thrived there.
key_features: Real-time action combat with jumping and weapon switching
World restoration mechanic where players revive dead land and creatures
Non-linear exploration with interconnected world map
Puzzle-solving elements integrated into dungeon navigation
Character customization through equipment and stat growth
Unique 'Mud' mechanic allowing transformation of terrain
Seamless transition between overworld and dungeons
Rich narrative delivered through environmental storytelling
legacy_and_impact: Terranigma is widely regarded as one of the greatest Action RPGs of the 16-bit era, often cited alongside Chrono Trigger and Secret of Mana. Its innovative world-restoration gameplay and stunning visuals have cemented its status as a cult classic, influencing modern indie titles focused on environmental themes.
